newsSince: Mitchell P. Rales appointed Executive Chair on June 10, formalizing the Rales/Kambeyanda leadership alignment. DA Davidson initiated coverage with a Buy and $130 PT on June 16, citing margin and accretion from the Eddyfi deal (closed June 2). Stock is +13.9% since June 9.

sources.label: ESAB names Executive Chair (Globe and Mail)
ESAB rallies as Eddyfi deal fuels bullish target (StocksToTrade)

verdicts.rankRationale: Genuinely new since June 9: Rales Executive Chair (Jun 10) and DA Davidson Buy/$130 PT (Jun 16). Eddyfi (NDT inspection, +$5B TAM) deepens the welding/fabrication latent-node case for reshoring. Modest positive; +14% move is price re-rating, not thesis change, so small score bump only.
verdicts.bull: Eddyfi adds $270M rev/$80M EBITDA and a $5B inspection TAM; sell-side initiating Buy; leadership aligned for transformation.
verdicts.neutral: Accretion real but priced in after +14% pop; updated FY guidance not yet given (next earnings call).
verdicts.bear: Late-cycle welding demand; integration/equity-financing dilution; latent-node link to compute is loose and indirect.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 earnings call with updated FY guidance incorporating Eddyfi
verdicts.primaryRisk: Industrial demand softening and integration execution
